iter1.summary: Iteration 1's headline 29.5% accuracy is dominated by two mechanical defects, not agent reasoning quality. First, a citation-rejection budget spiral: search_filing only shows the model a whitespace-collapsed snippet (tools.py:59 `" ".join(text.split())`, payload key `snippet` at tools.py:79), but _normalize_citation validates with an exact `chunk.text.find(quote)` against raw newline/\xa0-laden PDF text (agent.py:282). The model copies quotes from what it sees and can never match, producing all 102 citation_quote_not_verbatim rejections (0 chunk-id hallucinations). Because `tool_calls_used += 1` charges every action before dispatch (agent.py:594), each rejected record_answer burns one of 12 budget slots; 15 of 17 abstentions sum tool-calls + rejections to exactly 12, and for 13 of them calculate had already produced a grounded number before the first rejection. This one root cause explains the 102 rejections, all 17 (answerable) abstentions, and much of the 634s p95 wall and 4.6M prompt tokens. Second, a dataset-labeling measurement artifact: 44/61 golds have gold_value=null and 37 contain digits, so numeric answers are graded by normalized string-match instead of ±1% numeric. 27 of 31 answered-but-wrong items are substantively correct and fail only on terse-vs-verbose or number-format string mismatch; true content error rate on answered items is only 4/44 (amd_01 quick-ratio numerator, boeing_06 sign-flipped tax rate, pepsico_06 note-level restructuring miss, pfizer_04 Upjohn spinoff retrieval miss). Third, the LLM judge is non-informative: groundedness and actionability are both a flat 5.0 with zero variance across all 61 items (including 17 known-bad abstentions) in both iter1 and baseline, because the verbatim-citation gate engineers out the only mismatch groundedness can detect and actionability grades only fluency. Highest ROI for iter2 is (1) break the spiral in the agent loop — a real behavior + latency + token win unlocking ~15 answers, then (2) the eval_measurement gold re-annotation unlocking ~27 mislabeled-correct answers, then retrieval/derivation hardening for the 4 real misses, then a judge redesign to restore signal. The spiral fix and gold parsing are independent and compounding; both must land for measured accuracy to reflect the true ~91% content accuracy on answered items.

improvements.title: Break the citation-rejection budget spiral: whitespace-tolerant verbatim matcher, stop charging rejected record_answer to budget, and let citations resolve against fetched get_pages text
[EVAL-MEASUREMENT - forces re-scoring all iterations] Parse gold_value/gold_unit for the 37 numeric text golds so the numeric plus/minus 1% scorer applies instead of string-match
Retrieval coverage + stall guard + anti-confident-negative to fix the 2 pure search-loop exhaustions and 2 note-level content misses
Calculator/derivation hardening for ratios: canonical quick-ratio numerator, sign convention on negative denominators, and verify-recompute from cited line items
Restore judge signal: relevance/period-mismatch anchors, contrastive exemplars, abstention penalties, a hard-negative calibration fixture, and persisted per-item scores

iter2.summary: Iter2's headline accuracy of 41.4% massively understates true agent quality: of the 34 failures, 27 (79%) are substantively CORRECT answers blocked only by exact-string-match on prose text golds (evals/scorers.py:135). The single largest measured-accuracy lever is therefore eval-side: hand-annotate canonical gold fields (gold_polarity for 12 yes/no items, gold_canonical for 10 entity/choice items) and add matching scorer branches gated on those fields, exactly paralleling the already-accepted IMP-2 gold_value work. This unlocks ~22 items, lifting correct 24->~46 and accuracy ~41%->~75%, with zero agent changes and without weakening any existing scorer (exact-match remains the fallback; genuinely-wrong items like johnson_johnson_06 gold-polarity=No stay failing). The citation_precision 'regression' (61.4%->48.3%) is decomposed and NOT a true grounding regression: it is (a) a denominator shift as 14 formerly-abstained items became answered and (b) 7 genuine pass->fail cases (5 AMD) plus 20-of-30 total fails that are OVER-CITATION -- the agent cites the correct gold page PLUS extra non-gold pages, and the all-or-nothing scorer (scorers.py:153-159) fails the whole item on the extras; IMP-1's get_pages-citable change is the direct cause. A cheap agent-side citation-minimalism rule recovers ~20 items. The judge 'groundedness=5.0 zero-variance' is a MEASUREMENT bug, not saturation: Kimi-K2.6 burns its completion budget in the hidden reasoning channel, nulling 34/61 groundedness and 32/61 actionability scores non-randomly (killing exactly the long multi-claim answers where variance lives); live-proven that adobe_01 returns empty at max_tokens 4000/8000 but valid JSON scoring 3 at 16000. Raising max_tokens and re-running makes all judge numbers honest. Only 5 items are genuine accuracy defects (amd_01/verizon_02 quick-ratio definition, boeing_06 tax-rate sign, johnson_johnson_06 polarity, pepsico_06 'state 0' false-negative, boeing_02 branch selection) -- the confirmed-repeatable target for the deferred IMP-4. IMP-3 is re-scoped: only verizon_04 is a true stall the guard fixes; the hybrid/BM25 recall half has broader leverage (pepsico_06 miss + the 10 genuine citation gold-page/wrong-source-doc misses) than the stall-guard half.

improvements.title: [EVAL-MEASUREMENT - forces re-scoring all iterations] Canonical gold fields (gold_polarity, gold_canonical) + gated scorer branches so honest polarity/entity/choice matching applies to the 22 correct-but-string-blocked items
Citation minimalism: cite only the page(s) that source the reported figures, to reverse the citation_precision regression (20 of 30 fails are over-citation)
[JUDGE] Fix judge truncation (raise max_tokens), gate headline on coverage, add a long calibration fixture, and add a reference-based gold-agreement criterion
IMP-4 ratio/derivation hardening + targeted fixes for the 5 genuine accuracy defects (quick-ratio definition, tax-rate sign, false-negative 0, branch selection)
Scoped IMP-3: hybrid/BM25 retrieval for recall + right-source-doc, plus a Jaccard near-duplicate stall guard for the one true fixation stall

iter3.summary:
Iter3's TOTAL-CORRECT drop (46->43 of 61) decomposes into two independent, separately-fixable problems that the two substantive analyst reports pin down at item-level (the third "PRECISION + JUDGES" report is a placeholder stub with no findings, so it yields no action; judges already discriminate well and TOTAL CORRECT is the primary metric).

(1) Scorer false-negatives, not agent errors. Of the 11 residual wrongs, 6 are cases where the agent's answer is substantively correct (LLM judge gold_agreement 4-5) but scorers.answer_accuracy has no annotated branch to credit it and falls through to the exact-string fallback that free text can never satisfy. The scorer machinery (evals/scorers.py answer_accuracy: numeric branch gated on gold_value+memo.value, polarity branch gated on gold_polarity, canonical branch gated on gold_canonical, all ABOVE the exact-string fallback) already exists from IMP3-1; these items simply lack the human-derived annotation in data/gold_annotations.json. amd_01 already carries gold_value=1.57 but the agent returned a Yes/No text answer with memo.value=None, so the numeric branch never fires — it needs a polarity annotation instead. This is the single highest-ROI, lowest-risk lever: it is a pure rescore (no agent re-run) and lands ~+5-6 immediately.

(2) An over-search/never-commit behavior regression. All 4 true correct->abstain losses (boeing_01, mgm_resorts_01, pepsico_02, pfizer_03) share one signature: the model burned all 12 SUCCESSFUL search calls with ZERO record_answer attempts and ZERO rejections despite the answer-bearing evidence being present in retrieved snippets (iter2 committed on these same items in 1-7 searches). Root cause: iter3's citation-minimalism + verbatim-quote-strictness + get_pages-only-citable-when-quoted prompt changes raised the model's internal bar for a "citable" answer, so it hunts for a cleaner quote until budget runs out. This is NOT retrieval recall, NOT the recompute/zero/rejection-cap mechanisms (which fired on zero budget items), and NOT fixable by raising the tool-call budget (these items had zero rejections; more budget = more wasted searches at higher latency). The 2 "model_choice" abstentions (amd_05, boeing_07) went wrong->abstain and are TOTAL-CORRECT-neutral (a wrong "fail" and an abstain both score not-correct); best_buy_02 was already abstained in iter2 (pre-existing, out of scope).

The residual TRUE agent errors after annotations are only 3: pepsico_06 (retrieved Note 3/$411M but answered 0 on a literal "on the face of the income statement" reading — interpretation gap), boeing_06 (effective-tax-rate sign-convention conflict: agent's magnitudes match gold but signs flip on a pre-tax loss), and johnson_johnson_06 (genuine wrong-growth-basis miss; annotation already correct and correctly failing). pfizer_04 is contested/stale gold (agent's "Upjohn completed Nov 2020" is defensible) — keep skipped.

Plan orders the four levers by TOTAL-CORRECT-of-61 ROI. Net expected landing ~50-52, clearing iter2's 46 while preserving iter3's precision/quality gains (citation_precision 59.3%, discriminating judges), because none of the levers weakens a scorer or relaxes a grounding check.

improvements.title: Add 5 honest gold annotations (+1 flagged) to unlock scorer false-negatives on substantively-correct answers
Commit-nudge on the over-search/no-commit path + tighten the stall guard so it actually fires
Add two targeted reasoning rules for the genuine derivation/interpretation misses (pepsico_06, boeing_06)
Make the verbatim quote matcher case-insensitive to lower corpus-wide over-caution and recover amd_05/boeing_07

iter4.summary: Diffed iter3 vs iter4 memos, traces and llm_calls for all 7 flip items. KEY FINDING on determinism: the eval scorer is fully deterministic (evals/scorers.py, no LLM judge), so every flip is caused by the AGENT output changing, not scoring noise — and the endpoint itself is NOT deterministic. On all 11 companies the per-run "plan" LLM call had a byte-identical prompt across iter3 and iter4 (identical prompt_tokens: adobe 433, jj 479, pepsico 551, ...) yet completion_tokens differed in EVERY case (adobe 6394→4709, jj 3498→2219, verizon 2719→5319, ~2x swings; 0/11 identical). Temperature 0.2 + seed 42 is effectively non-deterministic on the Vultr/Kimi-K2.6 endpoint — the seed is not honored — and this is the churn engine. Classifying the 7 flips: (a) pure sampling/format nondeterminism — adobe_01 and boeing_02 (leading Yes/No token disappeared: iter4 phrased "does NOT have" / "is not a useful metric", so the deterministic polarity scorer found no leading yes/no and failed, even though the conclusion was right) and jj_04 (identical answer text and citation "Approximately $20 billion", but iter4 left value=null/unit=text instead of 20000/USD millions, so the numeric branch was skipped and it fell to the never-passing exact-string branch). (b) interaction with an iter4 change — amd_05 and boeing_07 were calibrated ABSTENTIONS in iter3 (not correct), and the commit-nudge flipped them to answered; amd_05's answer is a verbatim gold match (EPYC/semi-custom/Xilinx) but auto-fails because the item carries no gold_polarity/gold_canonical annotation, so any free-text answer fails the exact-string fallback — an annotation-coverage gap, not an agent regression; boeing_07 similarly auto-fails and is also partly off (cited 89% of BDS segment vs gold's 40% of total). pepsico_06 flipped wrong→abstain because the iter4 "notes-count-as-outlined" rule made it over-search Note 3 restructuring (25 tool/retrieval events vs 15) and hit MAX_TOOL_CALLS_PER_ITEM=12 → flag_outstanding. (c) retrieval nondeterminism — jj_01: iter4's differently-worded COGS queries surfaced the 2023 8-K continuing-operations COGS ($24,596, Kenvue-stripped) instead of the 10-K's $31,089; with the same avg inventory 11,435 that gives 24,596/11,435=2.15 vs iter3's 31,089/11,435=2.72 (gold 2.7, ±1% window [2.673,2.727]) — the wrong source, not arithmetic, left tolerance. Single highest-leverage knob for iter5: set temperature to 0.0 (seed is dead, so temperature is the only determinism lever) to kill the query-wording/phrasing drift behind ~4 of 7 flips; pair it with two cheap prompt-format canonicalizations (lead free-text judgment answers with "Yes"/"No"; always populate the numeric value+unit for numeric-gold questions) which deterministically recover adobe_01, boeing_02 and jj_04 regardless of sampling. amd_05/boeing_07 need a data fix (gold_canonical annotations), and jj_01 needs a document-priority rule (prefer the 10-K for FY figures over later 8-K/earnings restatements).

improvements.title: Answer-format canonicalization prompt rules (lead Yes/No; always populate value/unit for numeric answers)
Temperature 0.2 -> 0.0 (endpoint does not honor seed; churn reduction)
Generic geography synonym (North America <-> United States and Canada) + honest canonical annotations for amd_05/boeing_07; verizon_05 human sign-off
Judge model switch to DeepSeek-V4-Flash (bake-off: gate PASS, 9/9 agreement, ~30x faster)
